Name: Pineco
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It spits out a fluid that it uses to glue tree bark to its body. The fluid hardens when it touches air.

Name: Heracross
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each [C] in your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: With its herculean powers, it can easily throw around an object that is 100 times its own weight.

Name: Shroomish
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It prefers damp places. By day it remains still in the forest shade. It releases toxic powder from its head.

Name: Breloom
Flavor-Text: It scatters poisonous spores and throws powerful punches while its foe is hampered by inhaled spores.

Name: Cacnea
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It prefers harsh environments, such as deserts. It can survive for 30 days on water stored in its body.

Name: Cacturne
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Packs of them follow travelers through the desert until the travelers can no longer move.

Name: Tropius
Effects: Heal 60 damage from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It lives in tropical jungles. The bunch of fruit around its neck is delicious. The fruit grows twice a year.

Name: Scatterbug
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon scatters poisonous powder to repel enemies. It will eat different plants depending on where it lives.

Name: Spewpa
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.
Flavor-Text: Spewpa doesn’t live in a fixed location. It roams where it pleases across the fields and mountains, building up the energy it needs to evolve.

Name: Vivillon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a Special Condition. Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon was born in a land where flowers bloom. It scatters colorful, toxic scales from its wings during battle.

Name: Skiddo
Flavor-Text: Until recently, people living in the mountains would ride on the back of these Pokémon to traverse the mountain paths.

Name: Gogoat
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It can sense the feelings of others by touching them with its horns. This species has assisted people with their work since 5,000 years ago.

Name: Sprigatito
Flavor-Text: Its fluffy fur is similar in composition to plants. This Pokémon frequently washes its face to keep it from drying out.

Name: Floragato
Flavor-Text: Floragato deftly wields the vine hidden beneath its long fur, slamming the hard flower bud against its opponents.

Name: Meowscarada
Effects: You may put an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon into their hand.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon uses the reflective fur lining its cape to camouflage the stem of its flower, creating the illusion that the flower is floating.

Name: Tarountula
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.
Flavor-Text: The ball of threads wrapped around its body is elastic enough to deflect the scythes of Scyther, this Pokémon’s natural enemy.

Name: Tarountula
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: The thread it secretes from its rear is as strong as wire. The secret behind the thread’s strength is the topic of ongoing research.

Name: Tarountula
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: The thread it secretes from its rear is as strong as wire. The secret behind the thread’s strength is the topic of ongoing research.

Name: Spidops ex
Ability-Effect: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost is [C] more.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each [C] in your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Smoliv
Flavor-Text: It protects itself from enemies by emitting oil from the fruit on its head. This oil is bitter and astringent enough to make someone flinch.

Name: Smoliv
Effects: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It protects itself from enemies by emitting oil from the fruit on its head. This oil is bitter and astringent enough to make someone flinch.

Name: Dolliv
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn’t happen.
Flavor-Text: Dolliv shares its tasty, fresh-scented oil with others. This species has coexisted with humans since times long gone.

Name: Arboliva
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may heal all damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This calm Pokémon is very compassionate. It will share its delicious, nutrient-rich oil with weakened Pokémon.

Name: Toedscool
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Though it looks like Tentacool, Toedscool is a completely different species. Its legs may be thin, but it can run at a speed of 30 mph.

Name: Toedscool
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Though it looks like Tentacool, Toedscool is a completely different species. Its legs may be thin, but it can run at a speed of 30 mph.

Name: Toedscruel
Effects: You may move an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon to 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It coils its 10 tentacles around prey and sucks out their nutrients, causing the prey pain. The folds along the rim of its head are a popular delicacy.

Name: Capsakid
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: Traditional Paldean dishes can be extremely spicy because they include the shed front teeth of Capsakid among their ingredients.

Name: Capsakid
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic [R] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Traditional Paldean dishes can be extremely spicy because they include the shed front teeth of Capsakid among their ingredients.

Name: Scovillain
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
If this Pokémon has any [R] Energy attached, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The green head has turned vicious due to the spicy chemicals stimulating its brain. Once it goes on a rampage, there is no stopping it.

Name: Growlithe
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It’s very friendly and faithful to people. It will try to repel enemies by barking and biting.

Name: Growlithe
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic [R] Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It’s very friendly and faithful to people. It will try to repel enemies by barking and biting.

Name: Arcanine ex
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Discard 2 [R] Energy from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Houndour
Flavor-Text: It uses different kinds of cries for communicating with others of its kind and for pursuing its prey.

Name: Houndoom
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Upon hearing its eerie howls, other Pokémon get the shivers and head straight back to their nests.

Name: Torkoal
Effects: Flip a coin for each [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It burns coal inside its shell for energy. It blows out black soot if it is endangered.

Name: Fuecoco
Flavor-Text: Its flame sac is small, so energy is always leaking out. This energy is released from the dent atop Fuecoco’s head and flickers to and fro.

Name: Crocalor
Flavor-Text: The valve in Crocalor’s flame sac is closely connected to its vocal cords. This Pokémon utters a guttural cry as it spews flames every which way.

Name: Skeledirge
Effects: Attach up to 2 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Skeledirge’s gentle singing soothes the souls of all that hear it. It burns its enemies to a crisp with flames of over 5,400 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Charcadet
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Burnt charcoal came to life and became a Pokémon. Possessing a fiery fighting spirit, Charcadet will battle even tough opponents.

Name: Charcadet
Flavor-Text: Burnt charcoal came to life and became a Pokémon. Possessing a fiery fighting spirit, Charcadet will battle even tough opponents.

Name: Armarouge
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may move a [R] Energy from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: Armarouge evolved through the use of a set of armor that belonged to a distinguished warrior. This Pokémon is incredibly loyal.

Name: Slowpoke
Effects: This Pokémon is now Asleep. Heal 30 damage from it.
Flavor-Text: It is always vacantly lost in thought, but no one knows what it is thinking about. It is good at fishing with its tail.

Name: Slowbro
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may move 1 damage counter from 1 of your other Pokémon to this Pokémon.
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: If the tail-biting Shellder is thrown off in a harsh battle, this Pokémon reverts to being an ordinary Slowpoke.

Name: Magikarp
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: An underpowered, pathetic Pokémon. It may jump high on rare occasions but never more than seven feet.

Name: Gyarados ex
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 180 more damage.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Buizel
Flavor-Text: It spins its two tails like a screw to propel itself through water. The tails also slice clinging seaweed.

Name: Floatzel
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: With its flotation sac inflated, it can carry people on its back. It deflates the sac before it dives.

Name: Alomomola
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: It gently holds injured and weak Pokémon in its fins. Its special membrane heals their wounds.

Name: Clauncher
Flavor-Text: Clauncher’s claws will regrow if they fall off. The meat inside the claws is edible, but it has a distinct flavor that doesn’t appeal to all tastes.

Name: Clawitzer
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: The cannonballs of seawater that Clawitzer launches from its claw are powerful enough to punch through tanker hulls.

Name: Bruxish
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It grinds its teeth with great force to stimulate its brain. It fires the psychic energy created by this process from the protuberance on its head.

Name: Quaxly
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon migrated to Paldea from distant lands long ago. The gel secreted by its feathers repels water and grime.

Name: Quaxwell
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon constantly run through shallow waters to train their legs, then compete with each other to see which of them kicks most gracefully.

Name: Quaquaval
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A single kick from a Quaquaval can send a truck rolling. This Pokémon uses its powerful legs to perform striking dances from far-off lands.

Name: Wiglett
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon can pick up the scent of a Veluza just over 65 feet away and will hide itself in the sand.

Name: Wiglett
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon can pick up the scent of a Veluza just over 65 feet away and will hide itself in the sand.

Name: Wugtrio
Effects: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, discard the top 3 cards of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: It has a vicious temperament, contrary to what its appearance may suggest. It wraps its long bodies around prey, then drags the prey into its den.

Name: Cetoddle
Flavor-Text: It lives in frigid regions in pods of five or so individuals. It loves the minerals found in snow and ice.

Name: Cetoddle
Flavor-Text: It lives in frigid regions in pods of five or so individuals. It loves the minerals found in snow and ice.

Name: Cetitan
Effects: This attack does 20 less damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Ice energy builds up in the horn on its upper jaw, causing the horn to reach cryogenic temperatures that freeze its surroundings.

Name: Dondozo
Effects: This attack does 50 damage for each Tatsugiri in your discard pile.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is a glutton, but it’s bad at getting food. It teams up with a Tatsugiri to catch prey.

Name: Tatsugiri
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic [W] Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Basic Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Put this Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.
Flavor-Text: This is a small dragon Pokémon. It lives inside the mouth of Dondozo to protect itself from enemies on the outside.

Name: Magnemite
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It moves while constantly hovering. It discharges electromagnetic waves and so on from the units at its sides.

Name: Magneton
Effects: This Pokémon also does 90 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: They’re formed by several Magnemite linked together. They frequently appear when sunspots flare up.

Name: Magnezone ex
Effects: This attack does 50 damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent’s Pokémon.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mareep
Flavor-Text: Its fleece grows continually. In the summer, the fleece is fully shed, but it grows back in a week.

Name: Flaaffy
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Because of its rubbery, electricity-resistant skin, it can store lots of electricity in its fur.

Name: Pachirisu
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can’t be Paralyzed.
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your Benched [L] Pokémon. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness.
Flavor-Text: A pair may be seen rubbing their cheek pouches together in an effort to share stored electricity.

Name: Rotom
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Research continues on this Pokémon, which could be the power source of a unique motor.

Name: Rotom
Effects: Put an Item card from your discard pile into your hand.
Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Research continues on this Pokémon, which could be the power source of a unique motor.

Name: Toxel
Flavor-Text: This selfish, attention-seeking Pokémon stores poison and electricity in two different sacs inside its body.

Name: Toxtricity
Effects: Choose 2 random cards from your opponent’s hand. Your opponent reveals those cards and shuffles them into their deck.
This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: The jolts of electricity it launches by violently strumming the protrusions on its chest easily exceed 15,000 volts.

Name: Pawmi
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: The pads of its paws are electricity-discharging organs. Pawmi fires electricity from its forepaws while standing unsteadily on its hind legs.

Name: Pawmi
Flavor-Text: The pads of its paws are electricity-discharging organs. Pawmi fires electricity from its forepaws while standing unsteadily on its hind legs.

Name: Pawmo
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Pawmo uses a unique fighting technique in which it uses its forepaws to strike foes and zap them with electricity from its paw pads simultaneously.

Name: Pawmot
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Basic [L] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Pawmot’s fluffy fur acts as a battery. It can store the same amount of electricity as an electric car.

Name: Wattrel
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: When its wings catch the wind, the bones within produce electricity. This Pokémon dives into the ocean, catching prey by electrocuting them.

Name: Wattrel
Flavor-Text: When its wings catch the wind, the bones within produce electricity. This Pokémon dives into the ocean, catching prey by electrocuting them.

Name: Kilowattrel
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Discard a [L] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Kilowattrel inflates its throat sac to amplify its electricity. By riding the wind, this Pokémon can fly over 430 miles in a day.

Name: Miraidon
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Much remains unknown about this creature. It resembles Cyclizar, but it is far more ruthless and powerful.

Name: Miraidon ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 2 Basic [L] Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Drowzee
Effects: Your opponent chooses 1 of their Benched Pokémon and switches it with their Active Pokémon. The new Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: When it twitches its nose, it can tell where someone is sleeping and what that person is dreaming about.

Name: Hypno
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, choose an attack from 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon in play and use it as this attack.
Flavor-Text: Always holding a pendulum that it swings at a steady rhythm, it causes drowsiness in anyone nearby.

Name: Ralts
Flavor-Text: The horns on its head provide a strong power that enables it to sense people’s emotions.

Name: Kirlia
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has a psychic power that enables it to distort the space around it and see into the future.

Name: Gardevoir ex
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a Basic [P] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your [P] Pokémon. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon. You can’t use this Ability on a Pokémon that would be Knocked Out.
Effects: This Pokémon recovers from all Special Conditions.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Shuppet
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, they can’t play any Item cards from their hand.
Flavor-Text: It feeds on the dark emotions of sadness and hatred, which make it grow steadily stronger.

Name: Banette ex
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, they can’t play any Item cards from their hand.
Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 60 damage for each Trainer card you find there.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Drifloon
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is whispered that any child who mistakes Drifloon for a balloon and holds on to it could wind up missing.

Name: Drifblim
Effects: Put 8 damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: It can generate and release gas within its body. That’s how it can control the altitude of its drift.

Name: Flabébé
Flavor-Text: This Flabébé rides a red flower. Immediately after birth, this Pokémon begins flying around in search of a flower it likes.

Name: Floette
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage, and heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon uses red wavelengths of light to pour its own energy into flowers and draw forth their latent potential.

Name: Florges
Ability-Effect: Your Pokémon in play have no Weakness.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon’s attacks do 30 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: They say that flower gardens created by Florges are constantly showered with a power that can heal both body and spirit.

Name: Dedenne
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It’s small and its electricity-generating organ is not fully developed, so it uses its tail to absorb electricity from people’s homes and charge itself.

Name: Dedenne
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It’s small and its electricity-generating organ is not fully developed, so it uses its tail to absorb electricity from people’s homes and charge itself.

Name: Klefki
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, Basic Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s) have no Abilities, except for Mischievous Lock.
Effects: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: In the past, noble families entrusted their vault keys to a Klefki. They passed the Klefki down through the generations, taking good care of it.

Name: Fidough
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is smooth and moist to the touch. Yeast in Fidough’s breath induces fermentation in the Pokémon’s vicinity.

Name: Fidough
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is smooth and moist to the touch. Yeast in Fidough’s breath induces fermentation in the Pokémon’s vicinity.

Name: Dachsbun
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can’t be Burned. Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s [R] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The pleasant aroma that emanates from this Pokémon’s body helps wheat grow, so Dachsbun has been treasured by farming villages.

Name: Flittle
Flavor-Text: Flittle’s toes levitate about half an inch above the ground because of the psychic power emitted from the frills on the Pokémon’s belly.

Name: Flittle
Flavor-Text: Flittle’s toes levitate about half an inch above the ground because of the psychic power emitted from the frills on the Pokémon’s belly.

Name: Flittle
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Flittle’s toes levitate about half an inch above the ground because of the psychic power emitted from the frills on the Pokémon’s belly.

Name: Espathra
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
This attack does 50 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It immobilizes opponents by bathing them in psychic power from its large eyes. Despite its appearance, it has a vicious temperament.

Name: Greavard
Effects: This attack does 10 damage for each [P] Pokémon in your discard pile.
Flavor-Text: This friendly Pokémon doesn’t like being alone. Pay it even the slightest bit of attention, and it will follow you forever.

Name: Greavard
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand. Choose a Supporter card you find there and put it on the bottom of their deck.
Flavor-Text: This friendly Pokémon doesn’t like being alone. Pay it even the slightest bit of attention, and it will follow you forever.

Name: Houndstone
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each [P] Pokémon in your discard pile.
Flavor-Text: A lovingly mourned Pokémon was reborn as Houndstone. It doesn’t like anyone touching the protuberance atop its head.

Name: Mankey
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It is extremely quick to anger. It could be docile one moment, then thrashing away the next instant.

Name: Primeape
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Some researchers theorize that Primeape remains angry even when inside a Poké Ball.

Name: Annihilape
Effects: This attack does 70 damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It imbues its fists with the power of the rage that it kept hidden in its heart. Opponents struck by these imbued fists will be shattered to their core.

Name: Meditite
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: It never skips its daily yoga training. It heightens its inner strength through meditation.

Name: Medicham
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s attacks. During your opponent’s next turn, that Pokémon can’t use that attack.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Through yoga training, it gained the psychic power to predict its foe’s next move.

Name: Riolu
Flavor-Text: They communicate with one another using their auras. They are able to run all through the night.

Name: Riolu
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: They communicate with one another using their auras. They are able to run all through the night.

Name: Lucario
Effects: If any of your [F] Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent’s last turn, this attack does 120 more damage.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Accelerating Stab.
Flavor-Text: It’s said that no foe can remain invisible to Lucario, since it can detect auras—even those of foes it could not otherwise see.

Name: Sandile
Flavor-Text: It submerges itself in sand and moves as if swimming. This wise behavior keeps its enemies from finding it and maintains its temperature.

Name: Krokorok
Effects: If your opponent has exactly 1 Prize card remaining, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Protected by thin membranes, their eyes can see even in the dead of night. They live in groups of a few individuals.

Name: Krookodile
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
This attack also does 30 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: After clamping down with its powerful jaws, it twists its body around to rip its prey in half.

Name: Hawlucha
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may choose 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon and put 1 damage counter on each of them.
Flavor-Text: Its elegant finishing moves—performed by nimbly leaping around using its wings—are polished in the forest where it was born and raised.

Name: Silicobra
Flavor-Text: Silicobra’s neck pouch, which can inflate and deflate like a balloon, gets more elastic each time Silicobra sheds its skin.

Name: Sandaconda
Effects: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. If you discarded any Energy in this way, your opponent shuffles their Active Pokémon and all attached cards into their deck.
Flavor-Text: It will expand its body as much as it can and then contract itself, blasting out sand with enough force to wash away a dump truck.

Name: Stonjourner
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: The elemental composition of the rocks that form its body were found to match the bedrock of a land far away from this Pokémon’s habitat.

Name: Klawf
Effects: This Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Klawf hangs upside-down from cliffs, waiting for prey. But Klawf can’t remain in this position for long because its blood rushes to its head.

Name: Great Tusk ex
Effects: Discard a Stadium in play.
This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Koraidon
Effects: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This seems to be the Winged King mentioned in an old expedition journal. It was said to have split the land with its bare fists.

Name: Koraidon ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach up to 2 Basic [F] Energy cards from your discard pile to your Basic [F] Pokémon in any way you like. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Grimer
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Born from sludge, these Pokémon now gather in polluted places and increase the bacteria in their bodies.

Name: Muk
Ability-Effect: Your opponent’s Poisoned Pokémon don’t recover from that Special Condition when they evolve or devolve.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It’s thickly covered with a filthy, vile sludge. It is so toxic, even its footprints contain poison.

Name: Seviper
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 120 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It sharpens its swordlike tail on hard rocks. It hides in tall grass and strikes unwary prey with venomous fangs.

Name: Spiritomb
Effects: Switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.
Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Knocked Out.
Flavor-Text: It was formed by uniting 108 spirits. It has been bound to the Odd Keystone to keep it from doing any mischief.

Name: Croagunk
Flavor-Text: Inflating its poison sacs, it fills the area with an odd sound and hits flinching opponents with a poison jab.

Name: Toxicroak ex
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During Pokémon Checkup, put 6 damage counters on that Pokémon instead of 1.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Pawniard
Flavor-Text: Pawniard will fearlessly challenge even powerful foes. In a pinch, it will cling to opponents and pierce them with the blades all over its body.

Name: Bisharp
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon commands a group of several Pawniard. Groups that are defeated in territorial disputes are absorbed by the winning side.

Name: Kingambit
Ability-Effect: Your Basic Pokémon’s attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Only a Bisharp that stands above all others in its vast army can evolve into Kingambit.

Name: Maschiff
Flavor-Text: Its well-developed jaw and fangs are strong enough to crunch through boulders, and its thick fat makes for an excellent defense.

Name: Maschiff
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its well-developed jaw and fangs are strong enough to crunch through boulders, and its thick fat makes for an excellent defense.

Name: Mabosstiff
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may switch out your opponent’s Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Mabosstiff loves playing with children. Though usually gentle, it takes on an intimidating look when protecting its family.

Name: Bombirdier
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon Tool cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: Bombirdier uses the apron on its chest to bundle up food, which it carries back to its nest. It enjoys dropping things that make loud noises.

Name: Forretress
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.
During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 50 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Its entire body is shielded by a steel-hard shell. What lurks inside this shell is a total mystery.

Name: Varoom
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It is said that this Pokémon was born when an unknown poison Pokémon entered and inspirited an engine left at a scrap-processing factory.

Name: Varoom
Flavor-Text: It is said that this Pokémon was born when an unknown poison Pokémon entered and inspirited an engine left at a scrap-processing factory.

Name: Revavroom
Ability-Effect: You must discard an Energy card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It creates a gas out of poison and minerals from rocks. It then detonates the gas in its cylinders— now numbering eight—to generate energy.

Name: Iron Treads ex
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 3 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Chansey
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It walks carefully to prevent its egg from breaking. However, it is extremely fast at running away.

Name: Blissey
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Your Active Pokémon recovers from all Special Conditions.
Effects: Move all Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The eggs it lays are filled with happiness. Eating even one bite will bring a smile to anyone.

Name: Zangoose
Flavor-Text: It’s Seviper’s archrival. To threaten those it encounters, it fans out the claws on its front paws.

Name: Zangoose
Effects: Switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot. This attack does 30 damage to the new Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It’s Seviper’s archrival. To threaten those it encounters, it fans out the claws on its front paws.

Name: Starly
Flavor-Text: They flock around mountains and fields, chasing after bug Pokémon. Their singing is noisy and annoying.

Name: Staravia
Flavor-Text: Recognizing their own weakness, they always live in a group. When alone, a Staravia cries noisily.

Name: Staraptor
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon.
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When Staravia evolve into Staraptor, they leave the flock to live alone. They have sturdy wings.

Name: Skwovet
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may shuffle your hand and put it on the bottom of your deck. If you put any cards on the bottom of your deck in this way, draw a card.
Flavor-Text: No matter how much it stuffs its belly with food, it is always anxious about getting hungry again. So, it stashes berries in its cheeks and tail.

Name: Greedent
Effects: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon makes off with heaps of fallen berries by wrapping them in its tail, which is roughly twice the length of its body.

Name: Indeedee
Effects: Search your deck for a card that evolves from 1 of your Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: In search of happy feelings—such as joy and gratitude—Indeedee bustles around, taking diligent care of people and other Pokémon.

Name: Lechonk
Effects: Switch out your opponent’s Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It searches for food all day. It possesses a keen sense of smell but doesn’t use it for anything other than foraging.

Name: Lechonk
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: It searches for food all day. It possesses a keen sense of smell but doesn’t use it for anything other than foraging.

Name: Lechonk
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It searches for food all day. It possesses a keen sense of smell but doesn’t use it for anything other than foraging.

Name: Oinkologne
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, during your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: Oinkologne is proud of its fine, glossy skin. It emits a concentrated scent from the tip of its tail.

Name: Oinkologne ex
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If tails, during your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Tandemaus
Flavor-Text: The pair sticks together no matter what. They split any food they find exactly in half and then eat it together.

Name: Tandemaus
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: The pair sticks together no matter what. They split any food they find exactly in half and then eat it together.

Name: Maushold
Effects: This attack does 70 damage for each of your Maushold in play.
Flavor-Text: The larger pair protects the little ones during battles. When facing strong opponents, the whole group will join the fight.

Name: Squawkabilly
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Green-feathered flocks hold the most sway. When they’re out searching for food in the mornings and evenings, it gets very noisy.

Name: Cyclizar
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Apparently Cyclizar has been allowing people to ride on its back since ancient times. Depictions of this have been found in 10,000-year-old murals.

Name: Cyclizar
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Flavor-Text: It can sprint at over 70 mph while carrying a human. The rider’s body heat warms Cyclizar’s back and lifts the Pokémon’s spirit.

Name: Flamigo
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Thanks to a behavior of theirs known as “synchronizing,” an entire flock of these Pokémon can attack simultaneously in perfect harmony.

Name: Arven
Description: Search your deck for an Item card and a Pokémon Tool card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Beach Court
Description: The Retreat Cost of each Basic Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s) is [C] less.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Crushing Hammer
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Defiance Band
Description: If you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent, the attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Electric Generator
Description: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck and attach up to 2 Basic [L] Energy cards you find there to your Benched [L] Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Energy Retrieval
Description: Put up to 2 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Energy Search
Description: Search your deck for a Basic Energy card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Energy Switch
Description: Move a Basic Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Exp. Share
Description: When your Active Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon, you may move a Basic Energy from that Pokémon to the Pokémon this card is attached to.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Jacq
Description: Search your deck for up to 2 Evolution Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Judge
Description: Each player shuffles their hand into their deck and draws 4 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Katy
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 8 cards. Your turn ends.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Mesagoza
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may flip a coin. If heads, that player searches their deck for a Pokémon, reveals it, and puts it into their hand. Then, that player shuffles their deck.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Miriam
Description: Shuffle up to 5 Pokémon from your discard pile into your deck. If you shuffled any cards into your deck in this way, draw 3 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Nemona
Description: Draw 3 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Nest Ball
Description: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Pal Pad
Description: Shuffle up to 2 Supporter cards from your discard pile into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Penny
Description: Put 1 of your Basic Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Picnic Basket
Description: Heal 30 damage from each Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s).
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Poké Ball
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Pokégear 3.0
Description: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Supporter card you find there and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Pokémon Catcher
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Potion
Description: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Professor’s Research
Description: Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Professor’s Research
Description: Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Rare Candy
Description: Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 2 card in your hand that evolves from that Pokémon, put that card onto the Basic Pokémon to evolve it, skipping the Stage 1. You can’t use this card during your first turn or on a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Rock Chestplate
Description: The [F] Pokémon this card is attached to takes 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Rocky Helmet
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon (even if it is Knocked Out), put 2 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Switch
Description: Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Team Star Grunt
Description: Put an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon on top of their deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Ultra Ball
Description: You can use this card only if you discard 2 other cards from your hand.Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Vitality Band
Description: The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 10 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Youngster
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 5 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Tarountula
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: The thread it secretes from its rear is as strong as wire. The secret behind the thread’s strength is the topic of ongoing research.

Name: Dolliv
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn’t happen.
Flavor-Text: Dolliv shares its tasty, fresh-scented oil with others. This species has coexisted with humans since times long gone.

Name: Toedscool
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Though it looks like Tentacool, Toedscool is a completely different species. Its legs may be thin, but it can run at a speed of 30 mph.

Name: Scovillain
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
If this Pokémon has any [R] Energy attached, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The green head has turned vicious due to the spicy chemicals stimulating its brain. Once it goes on a rampage, there is no stopping it.

Name: Armarouge
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may move a [R] Energy from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: Armarouge evolved through the use of a set of armor that belonged to a distinguished warrior. This Pokémon is incredibly loyal.

Name: Slowpoke
Effects: This Pokémon is now Asleep. Heal 30 damage from it.
Flavor-Text: It is always vacantly lost in thought, but no one knows what it is thinking about. It is good at fishing with its tail.

Name: Clauncher
Flavor-Text: Clauncher’s claws will regrow if they fall off. The meat inside the claws is edible, but it has a distinct flavor that doesn’t appeal to all tastes.

Name: Wiglett
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon can pick up the scent of a Veluza just over 65 feet away and will hide itself in the sand.

Name: Dondozo
Effects: This attack does 50 damage for each Tatsugiri in your discard pile.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is a glutton, but it’s bad at getting food. It teams up with a Tatsugiri to catch prey.

Name: Pachirisu
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can’t be Paralyzed.
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your Benched [L] Pokémon. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness.
Flavor-Text: A pair may be seen rubbing their cheek pouches together in an effort to share stored electricity.

Name: Pawmot
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Basic [L] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Pawmot’s fluffy fur acts as a battery. It can store the same amount of electricity as an electric car.

Name: Drowzee
Effects: Your opponent chooses 1 of their Benched Pokémon and switches it with their Active Pokémon. The new Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: When it twitches its nose, it can tell where someone is sleeping and what that person is dreaming about.

Name: Ralts
Flavor-Text: The horns on its head provide a strong power that enables it to sense people’s emotions.

Name: Kirlia
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has a psychic power that enables it to distort the space around it and see into the future.

Name: Fidough
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is smooth and moist to the touch. Yeast in Fidough’s breath induces fermentation in the Pokémon’s vicinity.

Name: Greavard
Effects: This attack does 10 damage for each [P] Pokémon in your discard pile.
Flavor-Text: This friendly Pokémon doesn’t like being alone. Pay it even the slightest bit of attention, and it will follow you forever.

Name: Riolu
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: They communicate with one another using their auras. They are able to run all through the night.

Name: Sandile
Flavor-Text: It submerges itself in sand and moves as if swimming. This wise behavior keeps its enemies from finding it and maintains its temperature.

Name: Klawf
Effects: This Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Klawf hangs upside-down from cliffs, waiting for prey. But Klawf can’t remain in this position for long because its blood rushes to its head.

Name: Mabosstiff
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may switch out your opponent’s Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Mabosstiff loves playing with children. Though usually gentle, it takes on an intimidating look when protecting its family.

Name: Bombirdier
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon Tool cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: Bombirdier uses the apron on its chest to bundle up food, which it carries back to its nest. It enjoys dropping things that make loud noises.

Ability-Effect: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost is [C] more.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each [C] in your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Arcanine ex
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Discard 2 [R] Energy from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Gyarados ex
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 180 more damage.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Magnezone ex
Effects: This attack does 50 damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent’s Pokémon.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: This Pokémon recovers from all Special Conditions.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Banette ex
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, they can’t play any Item cards from their hand.
Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 60 damage for each Trainer card you find there.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: Discard a Stadium in play.

ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Koraidon ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach up to 2 Basic [F] Energy cards from your discard pile to your Basic [F] Pokémon in any way you like. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Nest Ball
Description: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Rare Candy
Description: Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 2 card in your hand that evolves from that Pokémon, put that card onto the Basic Pokémon to evolve it, skipping the Stage 1. You can’t use this card during your first turn or on a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.
